jokes.text: I left a rusty hacksaw on the table, so the first person who hacks their face off gets release.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining the locked room situation with serious security measures
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion of self-mutilation as an escape method
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly creates excellent contrast. Economy is strong - gets maximum absurdity from minimal setup.

jokes.text: Game of Thrones is on tonight. It's the series finale. Khaleesi is marrying Jack Sparrow. Oh, God, that show has really gone off the rails.
jokes.setup: Ben panicking about missing TV
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical plot description delivered with genuine concern
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd fake spoiler that sounds plausible in its ridiculousness. Character integration works as Ben would genuinely panic about missing TV.

jokes.text: It makes sense if you've read the books.
jokes.setup: Ben's absurd Game of Thrones description
jokes.punchline: Andy defending the nonsensical plot as if it's legitimate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy response - earnest defense of absurdity. Maximum economy with perfect character voice.

jokes.text: Terry, look at me. Is this what you want to do? Think very carefully, son.
jokes.setup: Ron treating Terry like he's about to make a life-changing decision
jokes.punchline: The gravity applied to Terry's simple choice to leave them locked up
jokes.explanation: Ron's paternal disappointment applied to a trivial situation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: For once in your life, do something right.
jokes.setup: Both Leslie and Ron competing for Terry's allegiance
jokes.punchline: Leslie's harsh judgment about Terry's entire life over this moment
jokes.explanation: Leslie's characteristic over-the-top emotional investment in minor situations.

jokes.text: Damn it, Terry! [both say it simultaneously]
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ron fighting over Terry
jokes.punchline: Their simultaneous frustration reveals they're more alike than they admit
jokes.explanation: Perfect moment of unintentional unity showing their fundamental similarity despite the conflict.

jokes.text: I'll simply punch my fist through a window.
jokes.setup: Leslie looking for logical escape routes
jokes.punchline: Ron's immediate jump to violence as a solution
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron - immediate resort to self-harm rather than emotional vulnerability. Pure character comedy.

jokes.text: I would rather bleed out than sit here and talk about my feelings for ten hours.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining the security wire danger
jokes.punchline: Ron's genuine preference for death over emotional conversation
jokes.explanation: Peak Ron Swanson - would literally rather die than discuss emotions. Dark but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I'll just tell them that I apologized for... whatever, heroically caring too much
jokes.setup: Leslie planning their fake reconciliation story
jokes.punchline: Leslie's inability to admit fault, even in a fictional scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - even her fake apology becomes self-congratulatory. Great character insight.

jokes.text: Because we want them to believe us and not laugh really hard at a ridiculous science fiction scenario.
jokes.setup: Ron questioning why he has to be the problem
jokes.punchline: Leslie calling Ron being reasonable 'science fiction'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant burn disguised as practical reasoning. The 'science fiction' comparison is both harsh and funny.

jokes.text: Fine! I'll tell them that for the past three years, you've been a perfect gentleman and scholar, and I am an insane weirdo who, despite being the only reason we even had a friendship to begin with, decided to ruin it out of the blue for no good reason.
jokes.setup: Ron insisting on not being blamed
jokes.punchline: Leslie's sarcastic over-the-top self-blame that still manages to be self-congratulatory
jokes.explanation: Leslie can't even fake self-deprecation without making herself the hero. Perfect character writing.

jokes.text: That sounds good. Let's go with that.
jokes.setup: Leslie's obviously sarcastic self-blame
jokes.punchline: Ron taking it at face value and agreeing
jokes.explanation: Ron's deadpan acceptance of Leslie's sarcasm shows either obtuseness or subtle trolling. Perfect timing.

jokes.text: [Leslie shouts and destroys the monitor]
jokes.setup: Ron calmly agreeing to Leslie's sarcastic version
jokes.punchline: Leslie's explosive physical reaction to Ron's response
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing Leslie's frustration boiling over. Visual punchline to the verbal setup.

jokes.text: I did not consider that possibility!
jokes.setup: Ron asking about emergencies after Leslie destroyed their communication
jokes.punchline: Leslie admitting she acted without thinking despite being a planner
jokes.explanation: Leslie admitting to not planning something is against character, making it funny.

jokes.text: You, of all people, should know that, Lauren.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining he tried not to notice anything at work
jokes.punchline: Ron calling Leslie the wrong name despite claiming to know her well
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Ron's character trait, undermining his own point with perfect irony.

jokes.text: I'll take that bet at any odds, for any amount of money, over any length of time stretching from now into infinity.
jokes.setup: Leslie betting she can make Ron talk
jokes.punchline: Ron's elaborate, absolute commitment to silence
jokes.explanation: Ron's verbose commitment to not being verbose is beautifully ironic. Classic character comedy.

jokes.text: I think wood is stupid, and so does everybody else. You guys, Ron loves plastic.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to provoke Ron by attacking his core beliefs
jokes.punchline: Claiming Ron loves the material he most despises
jokes.explanation: Leslie knows exactly how to hit Ron's pressure points. Wood vs. plastic is perfect for his character.

jokes.text: You chose Buddy by Willie Nelson, a fact I remember because my mind is a steel trap of friendship nuggets.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her detailed knowledge of coworkers
jokes.punchline: The phrase 'steel trap of friendship nuggets'
jokes.explanation: 'Friendship nuggets' is a wonderfully weird Leslie phrase that perfectly captures her personality.

jokes.text: ♪ Harry Truman was a guy ♪ ♪ America, Red China ♪ ♪ All the countries, other people ♪
jokes.setup: Leslie threatening to sing 'We Didn't Start the Fire' wrong
jokes.punchline: Completely mangling the lyrics in hilariously simple terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly wrong lyrics that capture the original's complexity in childish simplicity. Very funny concept.

jokes.text: ♪ Joe Mantegna, Ian McKellen ♪ ♪ I have to buy a new toaster ♪ ♪ This is awesome, you're so stupid ♪
jokes.setup: Leslie's fake song lyrics continuing
jokes.punchline: Complete departure from the original with random celebrities and personal notes
jokes.explanation: The escalation from historical events to needing a toaster is perfectly absurd. Great commitment to the bit.

jokes.text: ♪ Freddy Krueger bought some pants ♪ ♪ Oprah has a turtle farm ♪ ♪ Peter Piper pee-pee poopy ♪
jokes.setup: Continuation of the fake lyrics torture
jokes.punchline: Descending into complete nonsense including childish bathroom humor
jokes.explanation: The progression to 'pee-pee poopy' shows Leslie's desperation while maintaining absurdist humor.

jokes.text: Stop this! I will speak with you for three minutes.
jokes.setup: Leslie's sustained musical torture
jokes.punchline: Ron's very specific, limited capitulation
jokes.explanation: Ron's precisely limited offer shows his control even in defeat. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: We were work proximity associates.
jokes.setup: Leslie claiming they were friends for ten years
jokes.punchline: Ron's clinical, emotionally distant description of their relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron phrase - clinical, bureaucratic, emotionally avoidant. Became a fan-favorite quote.

jokes.text: And once again, it is up to me to save our friendship, and I will do so using the most powerful tool known to man: a well-organized chart.
jokes.setup: Leslie declaring she'll save their friendship
jokes.punchline: Her solution being a chart, perfectly Leslie
jokes.explanation: Peak Leslie - solving emotional problems with bureaucratic tools. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: your utter lack of interest in maintaining adult friendships. - Work proximity associate-ships.
jokes.setup: Leslie criticizing Ron's friendship maintenance
jokes.punchline: Ron's immediate correction to his preferred terminology
jokes.explanation: Ron's stubborn commitment to his antisocial terminology even during an emotional moment.

jokes.text: At her request, the party's theme is 'Zombie Teenage Biker Gang Pizza Jamboree.'
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining April's party
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously specific and random party theme
jokes.explanation: Perfect April party theme - combining random dark and childish elements. Leslie delivers it matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: [British accent] 'Oh, dear. We live at the Morningstar. That's so posh and hibbley-fibbley-gibbley.'
jokes.setup: Leslie criticizing the apartment building name
jokes.punchline: Her exaggerated British accent with made-up posh words
jokes.explanation: Leslie's ridiculous British accent and invented posh words show her emotional state through humor.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, you made a key?
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to get Ron to continue talking
jokes.punchline: Discovery that Ron somehow crafted an escape tool
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron move - crafting an escape tool instead of engaging emotionally. Very in character.

jokes.text: The name of the store is 'Food and Stuff.' They sell food, and they sell stuff. If you don't like it, go to that new place, Complete Food. - It's called Whole Foods.
jokes.setup: Ron defending Food and Stuff's naming logic
jokes.punchline: Ron getting the name of Whole Foods wrong in the most logical way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron logic - 'Complete Food' makes more sense than 'Whole Foods' to him. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: I'm being held as a prisoner against my will, and I have the right, as a citizen of the United States, to blow a hole in that [bleep] door and walk out as a free man. It's in the Constitution.
jokes.setup: Ron justifying his explosive escape plan
jokes.punchline: Claiming the Constitution guarantees his right to blow things up
jokes.explanation: Ron's libertarian extremism applied to a mild inconvenience. Perfect character escalation.

jokes.text: There's no cursing in the Constitution.
jokes.setup: Ron's passionate constitutional argument with a bleep
jokes.punchline: Leslie's pedantic correction about language
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie pedantry - missing the forest for the trees in Ron's outburst.

jokes.text: [For He's a Jolly Good Fellow playing from the fake bomb with confetti]
jokes.setup: Ron's dramatic explosive escape attempt
jokes.punchline: Cheerful party music and confetti instead of explosion
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual punchline - dramatic buildup to the most anti-climactic, cheerful result possible.

jokes.text: You mean to tell me you've thought you had an actual land mine on your desk?
jokes.setup: Discovery that Ron thought the gift was a real weapon
jokes.punchline: Leslie's realization about Ron's decade-long assumption
jokes.explanation: Great callback revelation - Ron casually keeping what he thought was a live weapon for years.

jokes.text: [Leslie on recording] Congratulations, Ron. You've been doing an explosive job! [laughs]
jokes.setup: The fake bomb revealing it was a gift
jokes.punchline: Leslie's recorded pun about Ron doing an 'explosive job'
jokes.explanation: Typical Leslie pun, made funnier by Ron's decade-long misunderstanding of the gift.

jokes.text: Who gets angry at balloons?
jokes.setup: Ron's continued frustration after the balloon reveal
jokes.punchline: Leslie's incredulous observation about the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Leslie's logical point about balloons being inherently harmless and cheerful contrasts with Ron's anger.

jokes.text: Leslie Knope is an absurd idealist whose political leanings are slightly to the left of Leon Trotsky.
jokes.setup: Ron's old job application assessment of Leslie
jokes.punchline: Comparing Leslie to a communist revolutionary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron assessment - historically specific, politically charged, and probably accurate to his worldview.

jokes.text: I'll watch a foreign film. I'll talk to a man with a ponytail.
jokes.setup: Leslie desperately trying to get the janitor's attention
jokes.punchline: Offering to do things she finds culturally abhorrent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie desperation - offering to violate her cultural preferences. Both are hilariously specific.

jokes.text: You called me a heartless thug.
jokes.setup: Ron recounting Leslie's job interview tirade
jokes.punchline: The specific insult Leslie used that impressed him
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to her written apology, showing Ron remembered and was impressed by her boldness.

jokes.text: I would rather work with a person like that than with a milquetoast yes-man.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining why he hired Leslie despite her tirade
jokes.punchline: His preference for conflict over compliance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron logic - valuing honesty and strength over agreeableness. 'Milquetoast' is a great word choice.

jokes.text: Two years ago, you found out you were 1/4 French, and you had a nervous breakdown.
jokes.setup: Leslie guessing why Ron left
jokes.punchline: The specific percentage and extreme reaction to French heritage
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd guess based on Ron's personality. The specificity of '1/4 French' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Apparently, Craig is studying yoga. These are the only dry clothes I could find.
jokes.setup: Ron needing dry clothes after the sprinkler incident
jokes.punchline: Ron forced to wear yoga clothes, completely against his image
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy - macho Ron in yoga clothes. The explanation makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: I feel extremely angry.
jokes.setup: Leslie commenting on how Ron looks in yoga clothes
jokes.punchline: Ron's understated emotional response to his compromised image
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron deadpan - expressing intense emotion in the most understated way possible.

jokes.text: Stop whining, you big baby. I've seen you with your eyebrows blown off. I've seen you without a mustache. I've seen it all.
jokes.setup: Ron complaining about the yoga clothes
jokes.punchline: Leslie's catalog of Ron's previous compromised states
jokes.explanation: Great callback to previous episodes where Ron lost facial hair. Shows their long history together.

jokes.text: we needed a mindless factotum, and he's the best there is. - Amen.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining why they hired Terry
jokes.punchline: Terry enthusiastically agreeing to being called mindless
jokes.explanation: Terry's pride in being called a 'mindless factotum' is perfectly in character. 'Factotum' is a great word choice.

jokes.text: I was gonna ask you for a job. In the federal government. Just saying it out loud feels dirty.
jokes.setup: Ron finally revealing why he wanted to meet Leslie
jokes.punchline: His physical revulsion at saying 'federal government'
jokes.explanation: Perfect emotional payoff - Ron's anti-government stance making this admission genuinely difficult. Great character moment.

jokes.text: Sure, I loved shutting things down and bleeding the rotting beast from the inside.
jokes.setup: Ron reflecting on his government career
jokes.punchline: His description of his job as internal sabotage
jokes.explanation: 'Bleeding the rotting beast from the inside' is a perfect Ron metaphor - violent, visceral, and libertarian.

jokes.text: You big, fat, giant sap.
jokes.setup: Leslie learning about Ron's sentimental door-frame gift
jokes.punchline: Her escalating series of insults that are actually affectionate
jokes.explanation: Leslie's affectionate insults show her emotional overwhelm. The escalation from 'big' to 'fat' to 'giant' is funny.

jokes.text: For the first time in my life, I am present-less.
jokes.setup: Leslie wanting to give Ron a gift in return
jokes.punchline: Leslie's made-up word for not having a gift
jokes.explanation: Leslie's invention of 'present-less' is characteristically over-dramatic about gift-giving protocol.

jokes.text: Why does anybody in the world ever eat anything but breakfast food? - People are idiots, Ron.
jokes.setup: Ron's philosophical question about breakfast food
jokes.punchline: Leslie's simple, definitive answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Ron's food philosophy met with Leslie's simple agreement. Great character harmony moment.
